name: AORTOENTERIC FISTULA
definition: life-threatening condition complication of atherosclerotic aortic aneurysms WITHOUT previous aortic surgery / trauma complication of aortic reconstructive surgery / stent graft rare life-threatening subtype of intramural hematoma

demographics.Frequency: 3.5% of all deaths ← upper esophageal bleeding

dominant_finding: enhancing subepithelial esophageal mass with fistulous tract between aorta +
discriminating_features: extravasation of contrast material into bowel lumen (extremely rare but most SPECIFIC)
ectopic gas adjacent to / within aorta (KEY FINDING)
obliteration of fat plane between aorta and bowel
± hematoma in retroperitoneum / bowel wall or lumen
loss of fat plane between aorta and adjacent bowel, pseudoaneurysm, disruption of aortic

general: ectopic gas adjacent to / within aorta (KEY FINDING)
obliteration of fat plane between aorta and bowel
± hematoma in retroperitoneum / bowel wall or lumen
loss of fat plane between aorta and adjacent bowel, pseudoaneurysm, disruption of aortic
tethering / puckering of bowel wall toward aorta
hyperattenuating fluid collection / mass acute = hematoma / partially thrombosed
focal bowel wall thickening (challenging if involved bowel loop not distended)
extravasation of contrast material into bowel lumen (extremely rare but most SPECIFIC)
leakage of enteric contrast into periaortic space (rare)
CT: enhancing subepithelial esophageal mass with fistulous tract between aorta +
